trajectory.description: Observation: The CNN has 7 input channels (weight [32, 7, 3, 3]) but gets 3-channel input. Analysis: SB3 detects the image as an image space and transposes it from (H,W,C) to (C,H,W), so the observation_space['image'].shape becomes (3,7,7). But I'm using shape[2]=7 as n_input_channels. Fix: use shape[0] for channel-first format, and don't permute in forward since SB3 already handles it. Also need to handle the direction properly. Decision: Fix model.py to use shape[0] for channels and remove the permutation logic.

trajectory.description: Observation: Error 'Expected size 16 but got size 64' - direction tensor is (B, 4) one-hot encoded by SB3, not (B,) integer. Analysis: SB3 preprocesses Discrete spaces as one-hot vectors. So direction comes in as (B, 4) float tensor. I need to use argmax to get the index for embedding, or use a linear layer directly on the one-hot. Decision: Use argmax to convert one-hot back to index for embedding, or simply use a linear layer on the 4-dim one-hot vector.

Decision: Add explicit flatten/reshape before cat in forward method.

trajectory.description: Observation: 0% success rate after 2M steps training. The model is not learning at all. Analysis: Two likely issues: (1) predict.py loads PPO without custom_objects for the feature extractor - SB3 needs to know about MinigridFeaturesExtractor when loading; (2) The training might not be working due to the complex multi-task setup. Let me verify by checking if the model loads correctly and also simplify the approach. I'll use SB3's built-in MlpPolicy with FlatObsWrapper (minigrid provides this) which converts the dict obs to a flat vector - this is simpler and more reliable. Also fix predict.py to use the same wrapper. The key insight: minigrid has ImgObsWrapper and FlatObsWrapper built-in that simplify observation handling.

trajectory.description: Observation: 15M step training completed. Previous best was 0.1307 from 5M steps. Analysis: The model trained for 3x longer with a larger network. Need to evaluate to see improvement. Decision: Submit eval now.

Observation: Eval failed because predict.py treats --checkpoint as a directory but evaluator passes the full path '/workspace/checkpoints/final_model.zip'. predict.py does os.path.join(args.checkpoint, 'final_model') creating an invalid path. Analysis: Need to fix predict.py to handle when checkpoint IS the zip file path directly. If args.checkpoint ends with .zip or the path itself is a zip file, use it directly. Decision: Fix predict.py and resubmit eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: 15M model (0.0763) is worse than 5M model (0.1307). The 5M model had FourRooms 56%, DoorKey-16x16 28%. The 15M model regressed significantly. Analysis: The larger network (512,512,256) with ent_coef=0.05 likely caused training instability or the model overfit. The best checkpoint is ppo_minigrid_5000000_steps.zip. KeyCorridorS3R3, LockedRoom, MultiRoom-N6, Fetch all remain at 0% - these need specific skills. Decision: Resume from the 5M checkpoint with better hyperparameters - smaller network, lower learning rate, moderate entropy. Also try to improve by training more carefully. With 4.6h left and ~13 iterations, I should train for ~10M more steps from the 5M checkpoint. Let me also check what the 5M checkpoint architecture was - it used net_arch=[dict(pi=[256,256], vf=[256,256])] from iter 10. I'll resume from that and train carefully.

trajectory.error_summary: [TIMEOUT] Command exceeded 9076s limit. Consider reducing epochs or using a smaller model.
trajectory.description: Observation: Best model is 5M steps (0.1307). 15M resumed model regressed to 0.0763. KeyCorridorS3R3, LockedRoom, MultiRoom-N6, Fetch all 0%. Analysis: The agent needs to learn key pickup and door toggle. Higher entropy should help exploration. The 5M model was trained with ent_coef=0.05. Let me train fresh for 20M steps with ent_coef=0.1 to encourage more exploration of pickup/toggle actions. With 2.5h left, I can fit one 20M step run. Decision: Train fresh 20M steps with higher entropy, same FlatObsWrapper MLP architecture.
